question: Which failures in the frozen producer corpus can be fixed without inference or a broader MusicXML envelope?

scope.statement: This is an exact-artifact census, not a MuseScore-version or MusicXML-compatibility rate.

resource_boundary.before: With D=2**332 (100 decimal digits), divisions=D and four duration tokens [1,D,D,2D-1] sum to a valid 4/4 measure. importer 0.2 returned ImportSuccess whose first note duration was 1/D with a 333-bit denominator; snapshot_music_ir then rejected that IR.
resource_boundary.after: Every success is snapshot_music_ir-valid. Reduced numerator and denominator components are each limited to 256 bits before music21 for both lexical decimals and derived note onsets/durations, coalesced tie durations, chord onsets, and total duration; raw or derived 257-bit and 333-bit components return INPUT_LIMIT_EXCEEDED.

security_boundary.semantic_authority: The complete raw tree is resource-checked and fail-closed preflighted before any music21 call. Raw exact events, warnings, and normalized metadata remain authoritative.
security_boundary.third_party_projection: Only after a zero-error preflight, the importer reconstructs a fixed single-part, sequential-measure event XML containing divisions, harmony root/kind, and note/rest/duration/tie. Credits, instruments/MIDI, layout/print, lyrics/voice, key visual metadata, and legal extra non-note-bearing parts are absent from music21 input.
security_boundary.success_domain_statement: The only new sounding-semantic success domain is a completely absent mode element on a traditional MusicXML 4.0 key. Visual values already covered by the frozen loss policy no longer succeed or fail according to incidental music21 parsing, so the byte-level success set can also change without a sounding-semantic expansion.
pre_adapter_gates: External image/resource elements and xlink:href fail before music21, including inside otherwise ignored visual subtrees.
Authoritative note, pitch, harmony, tempo, divisions, time, and staff scalars reject ambiguous repetition.
Small integers use bounded ASCII XSD lexing; numeric normalization trims only XML whitespace, not NBSP or other Unicode whitespace.
Traditional keys require the exact fifths then optional mode element-only shape, only documented visual key attributes, canonical single-staff selection, and no mixed character data.
Part identity selects exactly one note-bearing part with one matching direct score-part while preserving and then projecting away legal extra non-note-bearing parts.
Raw and derived exact Fractions, location scalars, and diagnostic floods have fixed limits.
security_boundary.post_adapter_gates: Every result is snapshotted before validate_ir. Adapter exceptions expose only a fixed category and exception type. validate_ir mapping preserves at most 256 violations and adds one INPUT_LIMIT_EXCEEDED sentinel at the 257 boundary.
security_boundary.independent_review: Security/resource review: 0 blocker, 0 important, 2 non-blocking maintenance-script minors. Scope implementation review: 0 blocker and no unrelated semantic narrowing; its sole important finding was this documentation update.
maintenance_script_minors: The trusted local fixture generator subprocess still has no timeout or captured-output cap.
The census replay command relies on the separately tested manifest validator instead of revalidating schema, hashes, and basenames itself.

reproduction.command: uv run --isolated --with defusedxml==0.7.1 --with music21==10.5.0 python scripts/generate_producer_fixtures.py --output-dir $FRESH_DIR --musescore /opt/homebrew/bin/mscore
reproduction.legacy_policy: The original three artifacts are immutable inputs. The generator copies their exact bytes only after hash, source binding, importer semantics, and complete manifest rows match.
reproduction.same_day_observation: On 2026-07-16 the six re-exported XML raw/root/semantic hashes matched 6/6. This was a same-day observation, not a cross-day reproducibility claim.

cross_day_replay.environment: arm64 macOS 26.5.2 (25F84), CPython 3.11.15, defusedxml 0.7.1, music21 10.5.0, MuseScore4 4.7.4
cross_day_replay.observed_xml_difference: Every re-exported XML and the MXL root changed only encoding-date and miscellaneous-field name=creationDate from 2026-07-16 to 2026-07-17.
cross_day_replay.mxl_difference: The MXL root changed for the same embedded producer dates; the raw archive also changed ZIP metadata.

reproduction.reproducibility_conclusion: Fresh producer outputs are preserved exactly. Semantic hashes were stable across the observed date boundary, but XML raw/root bytes and MXL raw/root bytes are date-sensitive exact evidence, not reproducible-build artifacts.
reproduction.musescore_exit_note: MuseScore 4.7.4 on this host variably returned 0 or shell-normalized 134 after complete output. Nonzero teardown is accepted only after complete XML/ZIP validation, exact exporter marker validation, source immutability, and recording the actual exit code.

causal_check.diagnostic_only_mutation: In-memory insertion of <mode>major</mode> made each omitted-mode failure match its source for notes, chords, meter, tempo, duration, title, and license. The mutation was never frozen or used as imported evidence.
causal_check.implemented_behavior: The importer does not insert or infer a mode. It preserves mode=unprovided and emits a located warning.
causal_check.source_to_output_gate: For each source-bound artifact, tests compare source and output semantics. Omitted-mode rows may differ only in key descriptor and expected warnings; the explicit-minor row must be identical.

limitations: Only the exact frozen artifacts and exact producer versions are demonstrated.

The only newly accepted sounding-semantic case is an omitted mode on a MusicXML 4.0 traditional key signature; MusicXML 3.1 omitted mode remains fail-closed in this release. Byte-level acceptance may also change for visual metadata covered by the frozen loss policy and removed from the music21 projection.
No major/minor mode is inferred from harmony, note spelling, melody, or music21.
Multiple note-bearing parts, staves, or voices; key changes; nontraditional keys; other modes; navigation; pickup; non-4/4 meters; MIDI; and audio remain outside this compatibility claim.
A successful model-relative GREEN result is not evidence that an arbitrary real player can perform the arrangement.
